name: Himalayan pink salt in morning water
dose: 1/4 tsp in 500 ml water on waking
rationale: Fastest-acting electrolyte top-up on keto. Blunts keto-flu headache / fatigue / brain-fog caused by sodium + water dump during glycogen depletion.
food_equivalent: Sea salt is equivalent; food-only covers <1/3 of keto sodium need
notes: Widely available in Nepal; also doubles as morning hydration cue

name: Total daily sodium
rationale: Keto excretes sodium 40-50% faster than carb diets. Under 3 g/day is the single most common cause of persistent keto flu.
food_equivalent: ~1.5 tsp table salt/day on top of food salting
notes: Salt liberally; add bone broth; use pink salt water as a second dose before workouts

name: Potassium (food-first + supplement if low)
rationale: Cramps, palpitations, constipation all track with low K on keto. Avocado (~700 mg), spinach (~500 mg/serve) do most of the lifting.
notes: Potassium chloride (NoSalt/LoSalt) useful top-up; max 1000 mg/dose to avoid GI upset. Stocked patchily in Nepal pharmacies

name: Magnesium glycinate
rationale: Calming form; supports sleep, muscle cramps, insulin sensitivity. Keto depletes Mg faster than standard diets.
food_equivalent: Leafy greens + seeds (contributory, not sufficient at keto losses)
notes: Import typical via iHerb; glycinate rarely stocked locally

name: Magnesium citrate
rationale: Acceptable alternative to glycinate; mildly laxative which helps the constipation many keto users get.
notes: More commonly stocked locally than glycinate; split AM/PM if it loosens stools

name: Vitamin D3
rationale: Near-universal deficiency in lipedema patients; also supports insulin sensitivity and hormonal function on keto.
food_equivalent: Oily fish + sun (not at therapeutic dose)
notes: Pair with K2 MK-7 100 mcg. Take with fattiest meal of day.

name: Vitamin K2 (MK-7)
rationale: Directs calcium to bones away from arteries. Mandatory companion for long-term high-dose D3.
notes: Import via iHerb; usually combined with D3

name: Omega-3 fish oil (EPA+DHA)
rationale: Anti-inflammatory. Keto-for-lipedema reviews emphasize omega-3 loading to counter residual arachidonic / pro-inflammatory signalling.
notes: Check third-party purity (heavy metals); prefer re-esterified TG form over ethyl ester

name: B-complex (methylated)
rationale: Energy metabolism + red blood cells + nervous system. Thiamine and B5 demand rises on keto; B12 needs rise if red meat intake is moderated.
notes: Look for methyl-folate + methylcobalamin forms; cheap synthetic B-complex is common locally but less absorbed

name: MCT oil (C8 preferred)
dose: 1 tsp up to 1 tbsp per serving
rationale: Speeds ketone production; useful for fat-adaptation phase and for a pre-workout ketone bump. C8 (caprylic) raises BHB fastest.
food_equivalent: Coconut oil is ~50% MCTs but much slower and weaker
notes: Start low - GI upset is dose-dependent; brands vary widely, pure C8 is import-only

name: Exogenous ketones (BHB salts / esters)
rationale: Transient BHB boost. Does NOT substitute for dietary ketosis — the insulin-lowering mechanism requires carb restriction.
notes: Expensive, rarely warranted; consider for travel days only

name: Berberine
rationale: AMPK activator; improves insulin sensitivity. Useful when keto alone isn't fully reversing fasting glucose/insulin.
notes: Mild GI effects; don't combine with metformin without medical guidance

name: Creatine monohydrate
rationale: Low-carb + resistance training context: preserves strength and neurological function during glycogen depletion.
food_equivalent: Red meat / fish (1-2 g/day max from food)
notes: Local supplement shops stock it cheaply; flavourless powder fine

name: Packaged keto electrolyte mix
rationale: Alternative to compounding Na/K/Mg separately; convenient but expensive per dose.
food_equivalent: DIY ~= 1/4 tsp salt + 300 mg K chloride + 300 mg Mg
notes: LMNT / Redmond Re-Lyte popular brands - import only

name: Zinc picolinate
rationale: Supports immunity, testosterone, insulin signaling. Monitor copper balance long-term.
notes: Widely available; take separate from calcium / high-fibre meals

name: Psyllium husk (or ground flax)
rationale: Soluble fiber for the constipation many see in weeks 1-4. Also blunts any glucose spike from borderline foods.
notes: Sathi/ihalal brands available; take with plenty of water or causes worse constipation
